steps.title: Welcome to Day 2
steps.body: Yesterday you asked AI your first real question. Today you will learn to make your prompts sharper — because vague prompts give vague answers.

steps.title: Picture this
steps.body: You message a friend: 'Tell me about restaurants.' They will ask: 'What kind of food? Where? Budget?' AI works the same way — it needs specifics to give you something useful.

steps.title: Vague vs. specific
steps.prompt:
Vague: 'Give me travel ideas.'

Specific: 'Suggest a 5-day trip to Japan in November. I love food, quiet temples, and hiking. Budget is medium. No Tokyo.'

steps.title: The three-part formula
steps.body:
Clear prompts have three parts:

1. Your goal — what you want AI to do.
2. Key details — context, constraints, preferences.
3. Format — how you want the answer structured.

steps.title: Which prompt is clearer?
steps.question: Which of these would get a more useful answer?
options: Write a 200-word email encouraging a team to finish a project by Friday. Use a warm, supportive tone.
feedback: Too vague — AI has no idea what 'something' means.
Yes. Goal, context, tone, and format are all there.

steps.title: True or false?
steps.question: Adding more relevant detail to a prompt always improves the answer.
feedback: Think again — as long as details are relevant and not random, more is almost always better.
Correct. AI uses the detail to shape a more relevant answer.

steps.title: Spot the specifics
steps.body: Find the specific details in this prompt: Summarize a 3-page PDF about solar panel installation safety for a beginner. Give me 5 bullet points with simple language.

steps.title: Good fit?
steps.question: Explain quantum computing in one paragraph for someone who has never studied physics.
feedback: Yes — you gave the goal, audience, and format clearly.
Right. This is a well-structured prompt.

steps.title: One more to try
steps.body: Now try the opposite — a vague prompt. See if you can spot why it is not useful.

steps.title: Good fit?
steps.question: Do something useful.
feedback: That is too vague — AI has no idea what 'useful' means.
Indeed. This prompt gives no goal, context, or format.

steps.title: The same prompt gets different results
steps.body: Different AI tools can answer the same prompt differently. If you do not like the first result, try the prompt again or tweak your wording. Persistence pays off.

steps.title: Build a specific prompt

fields.label: What are your constraints or preferences?
How should the answer look?
fields.placeholder: a table with days and meals

steps.template: Please {{goal}}. Here are my constraints: {{context}}. Format the answer as {{format}}.

steps.title: Copy your prompt
steps.body: Good! Now copy this prompt and paste it into your favorite AI tool.

steps.title: Paste what AI returned
steps.body: Paste the AI's response here. We will keep it in your portfolio.

questions.label: How well did AI follow your instructions?
What would you add or change next time to make it better?

steps.title: Fill in the blank
steps.question: A good prompt has three parts: a clear goal, relevant ______, and a desired format.
feedback: That is right. Without specific detail, AI is guessing.
Close. The missing word is 'details' — the specific context AI needs.

steps.title: Match the problem to the fix

pairs.left: AI answered about the wrong topic

pairs.right: Include your constraint in the prompt

questions.question: Which part of a prompt tells AI how to structure its answer?
A shorter prompt with less detail usually gives better results.
If AI ignores your constraint, the fix is to make your _____ clearer.

steps.title: Day 2 complete
steps.body: You now know the three-part formula for clear prompts: goal, specific details, and format. Every time you use it, AI becomes more useful. See you tomorrow.
